title: nuphar × saijoensis
intro: Nuphar × saijoensis is a hybrid aquatic plant, specifically a cross between Nuphar lutea (the spatterdock) and Nuphar japonica (the Japanese water lily). The medicinal properties of Nuphar × saijoensis lie in its rhizome, which contains compounds such as nupharin and nupharonine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even have antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the rhizome of Nuphar × saijoensis is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
